chunks.page_summary: This page provides an article outlining the integration of Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM sensors with Beckhoff TwinCAT 3 controllers using the PROFINET industrial communication protocol.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

PROFINET Communication through TwinCAT 3-Beckhoff

Modified on: Mon, 14 Oct, 2024 at 5:16 PM

What products are involved?

SRV, SRD, DVP and DVM. Hence it can be used for any of these Rheonics sensors.

What is the purpose of this article?

To guide the integration of SME with a controller based on TwinCAT 3 using a Profinet communication protocol.

1. General Concepts

1.1 PROFINET Overview

PROFINET is one of the most commonly used industrial communication protocols, it enables data exchange between controllers and devices based on international standards. To link all the components in its network, PROFINET requires standard Ethernet as the communication medium. It can operate on higher transmission speeds than other protocols.

Figure 1. Rheonics sensor integration with PROFINET and with Beckhoff PLC.

1.2. Prerequisite:

TwinCAT 3 software installed https://www.beckhoff.com/en-en/support/download-finder/software-and-tools/ (https://www.beckhoff.com/en-en/support/download-finder/software-and-tools/)
key_facts: The article describes the integration of Rheonics SRV, SRD, DVP, and DVM sensors.
The purpose of the article is to guide the integration of SME with a controller based on TwinCAT 3 using PROFINET.
PROFINET is a widely used industrial communication protocol that facilitates data exchange between controllers and devices.
PROFINET operates based on international standards.
PROFINET utilizes standard Ethernet as its communication medium.
PROFINET can achieve higher transmission speeds compared to other protocols.
Installation of TwinCAT 3 software is a prerequisite for this integration.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Rheonics sensor integration with PROFINET and with Beckhoff PLC.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for connecting a Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) to a PROFINET system and installing the necessary GSDML files for TwinCAT 3 software integration.
chunks.extracted_text:
Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) with Ethernet and PROFINET enabled.
Rheonics GSD files (https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter) - TwinCAT 3 compatible GSDML File.
IP address of the Rheonics SME - this can be read/written out from the SME using RCP software
TwinCAT 3-compatible controller.

1.3. SME PROFINET connection
The Smart Module Electronics from Rheonics is PROFINET-ready if ordered. For that, the Ethernet RJ45 port is used. It can be connected directly to the client’s PC or through a router, switch, or hub.

Figure 2. Profinet communication diagram.

2. Installing GSDML file

2.1. Download the GSDML files from Rheonics Support Page
(https://support.rheonics.com/support/solutions/articles/81000401423-profinet-gsd-file-for-rheonics-inline-viscometer-and-density-meter), the file that you need is called GSDML-V2.4-Rheonics-SMET-TwinCAT-20220321.
2.2. Add the newly downloaded file to the path “C:\TwinCAT3.1\Config\Io\Profinet”, so the device can be detected within the TwinCAT 3 software.
key_facts: The Rheonics Sensor Electronics (SME) must have Ethernet and PROFINET enabled.
A TwinCAT 3-compatible GSDML File for Rheonics inline viscometer and density meter is required.
The IP address of the Rheonics SME can be read or written using RCP software.
A TwinCAT 3-compatible controller is necessary.
The Smart Module Electronics from Rheonics is PROFINET-ready if ordered.
The Ethernet RJ45 port is used for PROFINET connection.
The connection can be direct to a PC or via a router, switch, or hub.
The specific GSDML file needed is GSDML-V2.4-Rheonics-SMET-TwinCAT-20220321.
The downloaded GSDML file must be placed in "C:\TwinCAT3.1\Config\Io\Profinet" for TwinCAT 3 to detect the device.
diagram_labels: PC with TwinCAT 3 and RCP software installed

key_facts: GSDML files must be installed directly on the TwinCAT installation folder.
The document outlines configuration steps for PROFINET on TwinCAT.
The first step is to open an existing TwinCAT project or create a new one.
The Rheonics GSDML file is specifically identified as 'GSDML-V2.4-Rheonics-SMET-TwinCAT-20220321'.
Users need to navigate to 'I/O' in the TwinCAT device tree, right-click 'Devices', and select 'Add a new item' for configuration.

key_facts: The document details the process of integrating a Rheonics Sensor within a TwinCAT development environment.
A Profinet I/O Controller(RT) is added to the project by right-clicking on the 'I/O' -> 'Devices' node in the Solution Explorer and selecting 'Add New Item...'.
After adding, the controller appears as 'Device 1 (Profinet Controller)' under the 'Devices' tree.
The next step in configuration involves navigating to the 'Adapter' tab for the Profinet controller and using the 'Search button' to select the appropriate Ethernet card.
warnings_notes: The 'Error List' pane displays potential 'Errors', 'Warnings', and 'Messages' that may occur during the configuration process.

The TwinCAT software environment is used for this configuration.
Users need to select a network card from a list of available adapters.
The selected network card's description, device name, MAC address, and IP address are displayed.
The configuration involves selecting the 'OS (NDIS)' option for the network adapter.
A Freerun Cycle of 4 ms is specified.
The process includes scanning for PNIO devices after network card selection.
The 'Device 1 (Profinet Controller)' is an enabled component within the TwinCAT project.

key_facts: The page illustrates the process of scanning for and adding PROFINET I/O devices to a TwinCAT project.
The PROFINET controller creates its own network, which means its IP configuration on the settings tab may differ from the adapter tab's settings.
New PROFINET devices are expected to include three specific modules provided by Rheonics Sensors.
The TwinCAT environment allows for setting the IP address and other network parameters for the PROFINET network.
diagram_labels: Integration with Rheonics Sensor - TwinCAT XAE Shell
Figure 9. Scanning and selecting the Profinet I/O devices to be added to the project.
Figure 10. Setting the IP address for the Profinet network
warnings_notes: On the settings tab, it can be visualized a different IP configuration when compared to the one set on the Adapter tab, this is normal as the Profinet controller will create its own network.

key_facts: The document details the integration and configuration of a Rheonics Sensor using the TwinCAT software environment.
The Rheonics Sensor utilizes the Profinet communication protocol.
Modules and submodules for the Rheonics Sensor are organized hierarchically within the TwinCAT Solution Explorer.
All sensor parameters, including viscosity, density, temperature, and kinematic viscosity, are accessible via the 'Device Parameters' module.
A GSDML file (GSDML.V2.4+Rheonics-SMET-TwinCAT-20220321.xml) is used for device configuration.
The document instructs the user to create an additional task for linking variables from the Rheonics sensor.

The viscosity parameter is added as an input for a cyclic task.
The new variable should be selected as a Data Type Real, or another type if necessary.

The TwinCAT environment is running build version 4024.44 and configured for Release TwinCAT RT (x64).

3.13. It is necessary to also set the cyclic time to a power of 2 and sync the new task with the Profinet Controller. Go to Device 1->Synch Task and select the “Special Sync Task” option to be synchronized with the newly created task, the cycle ticks must be a power of 2.
key_facts: Figure 15 illustrates the process of setting the data type and name for a linked variable.
Section 3.12 describes how to link variables from a task to those created in the 'Device Parameters' module.
The 'Attach Variable (Input)' dialog allows for selecting specific input parameters such as viscosity and density median/mean values from device parameters.
Figure 16 demonstrates the variable selection process.
Section 3.13 mandates setting the cyclic time of a new task to a power of 2 and synchronizing it with the Profinet Controller using the 'Special Sync Task' option.
Build process shows '1 succeeded or up-to-date'.

key_facts: A valid cyclic time must be configured for the synchronization task.
All data from the Rheonics sensor will be displayed in real-time after activating the configuration and showing online data.
The TwinCAT Project2 build succeeded with 1 item, 0 failed, and 0 skipped.
Data visualization is also possible on the task input if the online mode is enabled.

key_facts: Data visualization from a Rheonics sensor is displayed within the TwinCAT software environment, showing a value of 1423.538.
Common PROFINET problems and their solutions are detailed in a dedicated troubleshooting article on the Rheonics support portal.

The Rheonics support portal offers related articles covering diagnostics, parameter readings with PROFINET, connecting PROFINET output, and downloading PROFINET GSD files for Rheonics inline viscometers and density meters.
Beckhoff provides fieldbus systems, including PROFINET.
